title: Probabilistic-Group DRO Over Soft Regimes
summary: Earlier hard worst-group ideas underperformed, but newer PG-DRO work argues that soft group membership is better when regimes are ambiguous.
rationale: Earlier hard worst-group ideas underperformed, but newer PG-DRO work argues that soft group membership is better when regimes are ambiguous. F1 pit states are naturally fuzzy near compound/age/phase boundaries, so soft memberships should be more stable than hard buckets.
implementation_hint: Define 6-12 soft groups from race phase, compound family, tyre-age band, and season-shift score using smooth kernels instead of hard cuts. Reweight training loss toward high-loss soft groups each round, or approximate PG-DRO with iterative sample weights on the current XGB/CatBoost stack. Compare against current adversarial weights under strict event-held-out CV.
expected_effect: Could improve robustness on shifted seasons/races without the variance hit of brittle hard-group weighting.
risk: If soft groups are poorly designed, this becomes noisy reweighting and can hurt the already-strong baseline.
